title: Did The Earth Move For You

jokes.text: Yes, Mom, I'm very happy with the holes that God gave me.
jokes.setup: Will reassuring his mother about not getting his nose pierced
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic reference to 'holes that God gave me'
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism that sounds innocent but has double meaning. Good character voice and economy of words.

jokes.text: I'd say so. Mom, no, I'm not making any extra work for Geoffrey. He hardly even knows I'm here.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he's no extra work for Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: The immediate irony as Geoffrey is clearly dealing with Will constantly
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom irony. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Yo, everything is Mop & Glo, Mom. They love me here.
jokes.setup: Will trying to reassure his mother everything is fine
jokes.punchline: Using 'Mop & Glo' (cleaning product) instead of standard phrase like 'A-OK'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific wordplay that shows Will's creative street vernacular.

jokes.text: Will! Excuse me, Mom, me and Uncle Phil are about to go for a run.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he and Uncle Phil are about to exercise
jokes.punchline: The visual absurdity of overweight Uncle Phil going for a run
jokes.explanation: Classic fat joke setup but executed with character specificity.

jokes.text: To heat something up, I have to be Lt. Uhura of the Starship Enterprise.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv complaining about complicated new kitchen gadgets
jokes.punchline: Comparing operating the microwave to being a Star Trek communications officer
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that captures the frustration with overly complicated technology.

jokes.text: but I am not a happy camper.
jokes.setup: Carlton building up to express strong feelings, warning about 'strong language'
jokes.punchline: The extremely mild phrase 'not a happy camper' as his idea of strong language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his uptight nature makes this understatement hilarious.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, I wuv you more! All right, you wuv me more.
jokes.setup: Will having a sickeningly sweet phone conversation
jokes.punchline: The baby talk 'wuv' making it even more cringeworthy
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy but gets the job done showing Will's lovesick behavior.

jokes.text: Isn't that romantic? He's quoting Elmer Fudd.
jokes.setup: Hilary commenting on Will's romantic baby talk
jokes.punchline: Pointing out he sounds like the cartoon character Elmer Fudd
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that perfectly captures the absurdity of the baby talk with economy and wit.

jokes.text: Hillary, you promised I could tell. -I know. I lied.
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining that Hilary broke her promise
jokes.punchline: Hilary's casual, matter-of-fact admission of lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - Hilary's complete lack of guilt is both shocking and funny. Great economy.

jokes.text: Everyone from the A list is gonna be there, which means... none of my friends are gonna be there, which means... they are all going to corrode with envy and hate my guts. I love it!
jokes.setup: Hilary excited about the People's Choice Awards
jokes.punchline: Her gleeful anticipation of making her friends jealous
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Hilary's shallow competitive nature with nice escalation.

jokes.text: That's ugly.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil reveals Geoffrey doesn't have a ticket to the awards
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's perfectly understated response to this slight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery. Maximum comedic impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Like his life isn't sad enough already.
jokes.setup: Family expressing sympathy for Geoffrey not getting a ticket
jokes.punchline: Will's brutal assessment that Geoffrey's life is already pathetic
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny observation that shows Will's blunt honesty about Geoffrey's situation.

jokes.text: No, Hilary, I bought a new set of Styrofoam.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking if they're having takeout again
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv's sarcastic response about buying new Styrofoam containers
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback showing Aunt Viv's frustration with the kitchen situation.

jokes.text: Gosh, Mom, who put the rusty nails in your granola?
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv being snappy and irritable
jokes.punchline: Hilary's creative way of asking why she's so grouchy
jokes.explanation: Creative and quotable phrase that's both specific to the granola-eating character type and genuinely funny.

jokes.text: It's being cleaned! Is that a problem?
jokes.setup: Multiple people asking about takeout food
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv's increasingly frustrated explanation that the lamb is 'being cleaned'
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation comedy showing mounting frustration with repeated questions.

jokes.text: Not that observant. Kathleen, your blouse is unbuttoned.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil praising Ashley for being observant
jokes.punchline: Immediately pointing out something obvious that Ashley missed
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure that undercuts the praise with immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm sitting in coach.
jokes.setup: Hilary complaining about the takeout food
jokes.punchline: Comparing eating at home to airline coach class
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary line showing her privileged perspective and standards.

jokes.text: Pardon me while I go get a knife.
jokes.setup: Everyone talking about how people would kill for those award tickets
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan suggestion that he'll literally get a knife
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment - dark humor delivered with perfect British understatement.

jokes.text: Actually, that was a puppy.
jokes.setup: Kathleen praising Will for cutting her steak in the shape of a heart
jokes.punchline: Will revealing it was actually supposed to be a puppy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates the romantic moment with Will's artistic limitations.

jokes.text: This is the dinner table, not the back seat of a Chevy.
jokes.setup: Will and Kathleen being overly affectionate at dinner
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's specific comparison to a car backseat
jokes.explanation: Good parental authority line with specific imagery that captures the inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Don't come around here with no babies.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking Aunt Viv to discuss the relationship delicately
jokes.punchline: His immediate, blunt delivery of the main concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Phil moment - cuts through delicacy to deliver the core message with humor.

jokes.text: You don't have to worry about us coming around here with no babies. I mean, we don't even know no babies.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's concern about teen pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation that they don't 'know' any babies
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Will missing the point while being technically correct.

jokes.text: Now I won't have to slash your tires.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil getting Geoffrey a ticket to the awards
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's casual mention of his backup plan for revenge
jokes.explanation: Another excellent Geoffrey dark humor moment delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

jokes.text: This dress makes me want to puke. You can't even tell I have a figure. You're 13 years old. You're not allowed to have a figure.
jokes.setup: Ashley complaining her dress doesn't show her figure
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's protective father response
jokes.explanation: Good protective father humor that captures the parental anxiety about daughters growing up.

jokes.text: If you value your vision, you won't look directly into the rock.
jokes.setup: Everyone commenting on Kathleen's large engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Carlton treating it like a dangerous bright light
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor comparing the ring to something dangerous to look at directly, like the sun.

jokes.text: Oh, I just spent an hour chasing my outfit on the new rotating dress rack.
jokes.setup: Family asking why Uncle Phil took so long to get ready
jokes.punchline: The image of him literally chasing his clothes on a motorized rack
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy that combines the high-tech gadget theme with Uncle Phil's size for maximum absurdity.

jokes.text: Well, yeah, I wanted to keep up with the Joneses, honey, not the Jetsons!
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil reminding Aunt Viv she wanted to redecorate
jokes.punchline: Her comparison between normal neighbors and the futuristic cartoon family
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures the technology overload theme with a memorable pop culture reference.

jokes.text: No problem. We'll just hop on my dress rack and swing by.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil needing to go get the tickets from his office
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they travel on his motorized dress rack
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the previous gag into a new context.

jokes.text: Boy, this is a rare treat. I get to cruise Beverly Hills in a Volvo.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey being asked to drive the kids
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic excitement about driving a mundane car in a fancy area
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey sarcasm that highlights the contrast between his expectations and reality.

jokes.text: It's sort of like my lucky drawers, except I wear it on my hand.
jokes.setup: Kathleen asking what Will's lucky mitt is
jokes.punchline: Will's comparison to lucky underwear with the obvious difference
jokes.explanation: Functional joke that shows Will's casual way of explaining things through analogy.

jokes.text: Man, this door is like straight out of Blacula. It's like the '70s crawled up in here and died. Yeah, J.J. Walker's lips are probably around here somewhere.
jokes.setup: Will commenting on the basement's outdated appearance
jokes.punchline: The specific '70s references and vivid imagery of the decade dying there
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with specific pop culture references that paint a vivid picture of how dated the basement looks.

jokes.text: It's not me, Master Carlton, it's the road. It thinks it's an.... Earthquake!
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about Geoffrey's driving on the curves
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey starting to make an excuse but realizing it's actually an earthquake
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection as Geoffrey's excuse becomes the actual problem. Nice structure with the realization.

jokes.text: Baby, no, listen, no. The ground moved, okay? The ground. The ground moved. Nobody was bothering it, it just moved, all right?
jokes.setup: Will experiencing his first earthquake
jokes.punchline: His incredulous repetition about the ground moving unprovoked
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific panic that shows Will's East Coast perspective on earthquakes.

jokes.text: I'm a grown man sitting under a table hugging a teddy bear. I am not fine.
jokes.setup: Kathleen trying to reassure Will he's fine after the earthquake
jokes.punchline: Will's accurate assessment of his current undignified state
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-awareness joke that captures the absurdity of the situation with precise observation.

jokes.text: I'm a grown man sitting under a table hugging a teddy bear. I am not fine.
jokes.setup: Kathleen saying they're fine after the earthquake
jokes.punchline: Will's self-aware description of his current undignified position
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor. Will acknowledging the ridiculousness of his situation creates great comedy.

jokes.text: I may never see Zimbabwe. Never dunk on Michael Jordan. I may never see Rudy get married on The Cosbys.
jokes.setup: Will lamenting things he might never do if trapped
jokes.punchline: The progression from grand to mundane, ending with a sitcom reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that mixes serious aspirations with pop culture obsessions, showing Will's priorities.

jokes.text: Try it and you'll be singing soprano.
jokes.setup: Carlton threatening to slap Hilary if she panics
jokes.punchline: 13-year-old Ashley's threat to hurt Carlton in a very specific way
jokes.explanation: Good tough-kid line from the youngest family member showing she can hold her own.

jokes.text: But your spot is more comfortable. I know. That's why it's my spot.
jokes.setup: Kathleen wanting to switch spots in the basement
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt honesty about keeping the better spot for himself
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Will's self-interested honesty even with his girlfriend.

jokes.text: I put them in my little black purse. Oh! You wear contacts! Is that okay?
jokes.setup: Will asking what happened to her green eyes
jokes.punchline: Kathleen casually mentioning she removed her contact lenses
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship reveal joke about artificial enhancement, functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Then why isn't it growing out your scalp?
jokes.setup: Kathleen claiming her wig is 'real hair'
jokes.punchline: Will's logical question about where it's growing from
jokes.explanation: Good logical comeback that exposes the obvious flaw in Kathleen's explanation.

jokes.text: Wind, rain, cheese steak, these are things I know.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why earthquakes freak him out
jokes.punchline: Including cheese steak as a natural phenomenon he understands
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment that shows his Philadelphia roots by listing cheese steak with weather phenomena.

jokes.text: Because every time I come near you, the ground starts moving. I'm just trying to get back to Philly... where the ground stays still and the people move.
jokes.setup: Kathleen asking why he won't come near her
jokes.punchline: Will's irrational logic and the ground/people movement contrast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay contrasting geological and social movement. Shows Will's creativity even in panic. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It was either the knob or one of you.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey breaking the radio knob to stop Carlton and Hilary fighting over stations
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact explanation of the alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment showing his practical approach to stopping arguments with implied violence.

jokes.text: Philip, are you trying to put the moves on me? Yes. Is it working? Yes.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil being romantic by candlelight during the blackout
jokes.punchline: The honest exchange about his seduction attempt's success
jokes.explanation: Good married couple humor with straightforward honesty about romance.

jokes.text: It likes you. Well, honey, I hope it has a warranty.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's pulse monitor reacting to Aunt Viv
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv's practical concern about the warranty
jokes.explanation: Good innuendo joke that plays on both the device malfunction and romantic implications.

jokes.text: What's the magic word? -Now!
jokes.setup: Will asking for the magic word to open something
jokes.punchline: Kathleen's impatient response instead of 'please'
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the expected 'please.' Shows their relationship deteriorating into rudeness.

jokes.text: Okay, look, Kathleen, in the last three hours... you took out your eyeballs, your fingernails... your eyelids, and your hair. Now what else on your body can I get at the mall?
jokes.setup: Will cataloging all of Kathleen's artificial enhancements that came off
jokes.punchline: His question about what other body parts are store-bought
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that builds the list of artificial enhancements to an absurd level with a perfect punchline.

jokes.text: Yeah, your mom sucks breath mints, too!
jokes.setup: Will and Kathleen's argument escalating about breath mints
jokes.punchline: Will's nonsensical insult about her mother and breath mints
jokes.explanation: Escalation joke that shows how petty their argument has become, though the insult itself is weak.

jokes.text: Yeah, your mom sucks breath mints, too!
jokes.setup: Their argument escalating
jokes.punchline: Will's childish 'your mom' insult with breath mint callback
jokes.explanation: Shows Will regressing to childish insults under stress. Continues the breath mint theme.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, like who? Dumbo's mother?
jokes.setup: Will claiming many women find his ears attractive
jokes.punchline: Kathleen's specific Disney reference to mock his ear size
jokes.explanation: Good insult comedy with specific pop culture reference that's both mean and funny.

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry, but I couldn't find any Lee Press-On Ears.
jokes.setup: Kathleen mocking Will's ears
jokes.punchline: Will's comeback referencing fake press-on nails applied to ears
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that ties back to the artificial enhancement theme while defending himself.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, don't be dissing my bear! Well, don't be dissing my hair.
jokes.setup: Their argument reaching the level of attacking each other's comfort items
jokes.punchline: The parallel structure of defending teddy bear vs. wig
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows both characters defending their vulnerable items with parallel phrasing.

jokes.text: Listen to me, I'm starting to sound like my Uncle Phil. Next thing I know, I'm going to wake up bald and hungry.
jokes.setup: Will realizing he's being too mature and responsible
jokes.punchline: His specific fears about becoming like Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will's self-awareness and fear of growing up, with specific Uncle Phil traits.

jokes.text: All it took was an earthquake. You think something hit him on the head?
jokes.setup: Will admitting his parents were right about taking things slow
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil wondering if physical trauma caused this wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good ending joke that shows parental amazement at teenager actually listening, with Uncle Phil's suspicious nature.

tmdb_overview: Will's all-consuming romance with a new girlfriend shakes up the Banks' household, as does Mother Nature, trapping them together during an earthquake.
